definitions: A strategy in game theory where a player chooses to cooperate with another player, but only if the other player has cooperated in the previous round. If the other player defects, the Pitt strategy player will also defect in the next round.
A tit-for-tat like strategy, but with a focus on immediate reciprocity and punishment for defection.
examples: The company adopted a Pitt strategy in its negotiations.
Implementing a Pitt strategy can lead to stable cooperation.
The Pitt strategy is effective in repeated games.
